## What changed

- Fixed `ERR_NGROK_3004` when a synchronous shell request kept the API event loop busy and prevented ngrok from receiving a complete HTTP response.
- Moved synchronous shell waiting into a request worker so `/health` and other API endpoints remain responsive while commands run.
- Made the ngrok upstream protocol explicit as `http://127.0.0.1:<port>` in both the Python launcher and PowerShell runner.
- Added regression coverage for API responsiveness during command execution and for the generated ngrok command.

## Standalone downloads

- `GPT-Connect-windows-x64-standalone.exe`: Windows one-file executable with ngrok bundled.
- `GPT-Connect-windows-x64-standalone.zip`: zipped Windows executable.
- `GPT-Connect-linux-x64-standalone`: Linux x64 one-file executable with ngrok bundled.
- `GPT-Connect-linux-x64-standalone.tar.gz`: packaged Linux executable.
- `*.sha256`: SHA-256 checksums for release assets.

The executables open GPT-Connect in tunnel mode by default. A valid ngrok authtoken is still required for the first tunnel session.
